DISCOVERY 301, PLAINTIFF JAVIER E. MARTINEZ'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S PERSON MOST QUALIFIED AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. MARTINEZ MAY SET THE DEPOSITION UNILATERALLY TO OCCUR WITHIN 30 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. GENERAL MOTORS SHALL PRODUCE DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO THE DEPOSITION NOTICE'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION. THE COURT DOES NOT FIND SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION AND ORDERS GENERAL MOTORS TO PAY $4720 IN SANCTIONS TO MARTINEZ WITHIN 30 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLAINCE WITH CRC 3.1312. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion for summary judgment is granted. As a matter of law, plaintiff cannot establish causes of action one through three (Song-Beverly Act claims) (see word minutes for complete ruling).

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Apr-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JAVIER MARTINEZ', AN INDIVIDUAL Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents.

Plaintiff Javier E. Martinez's unopposed motion to compel the deposition of defendant General Motors LLC's person most qualified and production of documents is granted. The parties are ordered to meet and confer and set a mutually agreeable date for the deposition within one week of entry of this order. The deposition must occur within 30 days of entry of this order unless the parties mutually agree otherwise. If the parties are unable to determine a mutually agreeable date, Martinez may set the deposition unilaterally to occur within 30 days of entry of this order. General Motors shall produce documents responsive to the deposition notice's requests for production.

CCP 2025.450(g)(1) provides: "If a motion under subdivision (a) is granted, the court shall impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) in favor of the party who noticed the deposition and against the deponent or the party with whom the deponent is affiliated, unless the court finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The court does not find substantial justification and orders General Motors to pay $4720 in sanctions to Martinez within 30 days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in complaince with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
